results.format_valid: All 5 new exporters parse/validate against the real signed envelope public/sample-audit-report.json AND against {}/null/undefined/42/'x'/[]/hostile-typed inputs with zero throws across all 11 exporters. SARIF: version==='2.1.0', runs[0].results is array (22 results / 20 rules on real, 0 on empty), every level in {error,warning,note}, properties.signed===true. OSCAL: assessment-results.uuid is valid 8-4-4-4-12 (real and empty), oscal-version==='1.1.2', results[0] has 12 observations / 26 findings / 29 included controls, byte-deterministic across two runs. AIBOM: bomFormat==='CycloneDX', specVersion==='1.6', components is array (exactly 2 machine-learning-model components on real, [] on empty). Scorecard: body starts '# Agent Security-Review Scorecard' and lists ASR-1. ModelCard: emits HF markdown (3333 chars real / 2164 fallback empty), no banned word. Badge: renderBadgeSvg starts '<svg', includes 'kolm readiness'; empty -> grey #9f9f9f + 'n/a'. JSON.parse succeeded on every sarif/oscal/aibom body for all hostile inputs. EXPORT_FORMATS now contains all 5 new ids (11 total). Route layer (src/audit-routes.js:340 _sendExport) dispatches generically via FRAMEWORK_EXPORTERS[fmt] and reports EXPORT_FORMATS dynamically (line 1395), so new formats are served with no route edits; the module's renderBadgeSvg/toBadge are intentionally not route-wired (the route has its own local badge renderer).
results.tests: node --test tests/framework-export.test.js => tests 24, pass 22, fail 2 (exit 1). I re-ran this independently and reproduced the exact counts. Both failures are stale hard-coded expectations inside the test file (tests/framework-export.test.js), which is OUTSIDE the implementer's declared ownership (only src/framework-export.js was to be edited): (1) line 106 assert.deepEqual(EXPORT_FORMATS, [6 old ids]) now mismatches the spec-required 11-id list (diff shows the 5 new ids as the only delta); (2) line 395 local `expect` map has keys for only the 6 old formats, so iterating the now-11-element EXPORT_FORMATS yields expect['sarif']===undefined and assert.match(...,undefined) throws ERR_INVALID_ARG_TYPE. Both are test-fixture staleness, not defects in framework-export.js; the route itself serves all new formats. The other 22 tests pass, including never-throws-on-malformed, read-only, no-banned-word, and dev@kolm.ai-only.
issues: NON-BLOCKING (test fixture, not in changed file): tests/framework-export.test.js line 106 hard-codes EXPORT_FORMATS as the 6 old ids; update expected array to ['csv','xlsx','drata','vanta','exec','crosswalk','sarif','oscal','aibom','scorecard','modelcard'] so the extended list passes.

NON-BLOCKING (test fixture, not in changed file): tests/framework-export.test.js line 395 local `expect` map lacks keys for sarif/oscal/aibom/scorecard/modelcard, causing assert.match(undefined) at line 402; add content-type regex entries (sarif: /application\/sarif\+json/, oscal: /application\/json/, aibom: /application\/json/, scorecard: /text\/markdown/, modelcard: /text\/markdown/) -- or narrow the loop to the formats the route test intends to cover.

INFORMATIONAL (pre-existing, not introduced by this change): exec and crosswalk exporters emit U+00B7 middle dot on lines 627/683; the 5 NEW exporters and the badge are pure ASCII (verified). The hard constraint forbids em/en dashes U+2012-2015 and smart quotes (none present); U+00B7 is pre-existing on lines the implementer did not author and is not a violation of the stated constraint, but flag if strict ASCII-only across the whole file is desired.

results.format_valid: All 8 templates parse and run. QUESTIONNAIRE_TEMPLATES has 8 ids including the 4 new ones (ai-caiq, sig-core, vsaq, eu-ai-act-fria) plus extended eu-ai-act. Exercised every new id against the real signed public/sample-audit-report.json, against {} (empty), and against the raw JSON string: question_counts exact (ai-caiq 11, sig-core 12, vsaq 7, eu-ai-act-fria 7, eu-ai-act 9), every question got a well-formed answer in {yes,no,partial,n/a} with >=1 evidence entry, no throws, all answer/evidence/question strings ASCII, toQuestionnaireCsv ASCII and never-throws. Against {} every answer is correctly n/a. Sample (0-readiness dirty scan) yields sensible distributions (mostly no/partial; untested controls n/a). All ASR ids referenced map to the canonical ASR-1..ASR-8 set in control-mapper.js. Functions are pure/never-throw (asReport guards string/null/non-object; answerQuestion wrapped in try/catch; unknown template returns error object not throw).
results.tests: node --test tests/questionnaire-autofill.test.js -> tests 10, pass 8, fail 2. The 2 failures are the pre-existing hardcoded-count assertions (line 100 'lists the four built-in templates' deepEqual against the original 4 ids, and line 259 'unknown template' asserting available_templates.length===4). Both live in the test file, which the implementer does not own. Every functional/logic test passed (clean-report rollup, dirty-report cited no/partial/n/a, multi-control rollup, eu-ai-act article mapping, real runAudit->buildAndSignReport envelope, CSV escaping/ASCII, never-throws on malformed input, default template).
issues: NON-BLOCKING (test-owner action, outside B-quest's owned file): tests/questionnaire-autofill.test.js hardcodes the template count at 4 in two places. Line 22 TEMPLATE_IDS and line 100/103 deepEqual expect exactly ['sig-lite','caiq','eu-ai-act','generic-ai-vendor']; line 263 asserts available_templates.length===4. Update TEMPLATE_IDS to the now-8 ids (and optionally add the 4 new ids to the iterated coverage), and change the length assertion to 8. These are an expected consequence of adding templates, not a defect in src/questionnaire-autofill.js.

results.format_valid: Well-formed. mapControls(sample 12 findings) rolls up the 3 new frameworks (verified). mapControls({}) gives 0 findings, asr.length 8. Never-throws on undefined/null/42/x/empty-array/[null,5]/{id:x}. SARIF version 2.1.0 + runs[0].results array. OSCAL assessment-results.uuid present + results[] array. AIBOM bomFormat CycloneDX + specVersion 1.6 + components array. EXPORT_FORMATS has all 11 ids. toCSV/toSarif/toOscal include 27001 + 164.312 + 800-53. Crosswalk per-framework section surfaces all 3 new frameworks. Every claimed cascade reproduced exactly.
results.tests: Re-ran independently: tests/agent-control-mapper.test.js 8 pass / 0 fail. tests/agent-audit-pipeline.test.js 3 pass / 0 fail. tests/framework-export.test.js 22 pass / 2 fail; both failures are stale tests outside this domain (they hardcode the old 6-format list and do assert.match against expect[fmt] which is undefined for sarif/oscal/aibom/scorecard/modelcard, throwing ERR_INVALID_ARG_TYPE). Caused by EXPORTERS in framework-export.js, independent of control-mapper.js.
issues: NON-ASCII in changed file src/control-mapper.js (violates ASCII-only HARD CONSTRAINT; all hits in comments): line 10 has U+2026 ellipsis; lines 119 and 194 each have a U+2192 right-arrow. Self-report disclosed only the line-10 ellipsis and not the two arrows. Fix: replace U+2026 with ... and both U+2192 with -> for pure ASCII. Clean otherwise: no em/en dashes U+2012-U+2015, no honest, no forbidden cert substrings, no personal email.

OUT-OF-DOMAIN (not control-mapper; for main loop): tests/framework-export.test.js has 2 pre-existing failures because EXPORTERS in framework-export.js now exposes sarif/oscal/aibom/scorecard/modelcard while two tests still hardcode the old 6 formats. New frameworks propagate correctly through every exporter.

results.format_valid: Both connectors parse + normalize correctly and never throw. OpenInference: synthetic OTLP doc detected as 'openinference' BEFORE otel; normalize yields exactly 3 canonical AuditEvents (LLM model=gpt-4o host=api.openai.com, TOOL send_email host=smtp.example.com PII+egress, RETRIEVER kind=retrieval PII no-egress; AGENT span correctly dropped); request projection present. Langfuse: synthetic trace detected as 'langfuse' BEFORE langsmith; GENERATION yields model event (gpt-4o/api.openai.com) + 2 lifted tool_calls (send_email->smtp.example.com, charge_card->api.stripe.com), vector_search SPAN->retrieval event; trace user_id/session_id/tags propagated onto meta WITHOUT clobbering source/model/kind; actor.agent derived from user_id. Registry: SOURCES=[datadog,langfuse,langsmith,openinference,otel], both default exports {normalize,source} valid. Detector ordering verified non-regressive: datadog/langsmith/otel still classify correctly (langfuse does not steal langsmith, openinference does not steal otel). normalizeAuto + runAudit consume both connectors' events end-to-end (ingest.events>0, sensitive-egress finding surfaces for both). Never-throw confirmed across 30 garbage inputs incl {}, sample-audit-report.json, NaN/Infinity, malformed wrappers - all return arrays, {} and sample report yield [].
results.tests: tests/connectors.test.js re-run independently: 8 tests, 7 pass, 1 fail. The lone failure is the pre-existing lock-in test 'registry exposes the three connectors' (line 150-151) which hard-codes SOURCES deepEqual [datadog,langsmith,otel]; it now correctly observes all 5 connectors. This test is outside the 3 owned files. Separately, a 164-assertion adversarial harness I wrote and ran returned 164 pass / 0 fail.
issues: TEST-OWNER FOLLOW-UP (not a defect in the 3 owned files): tests/connectors.test.js line 150-151 must be updated - change assert.deepEqual([...SOURCES].sort(), ['datadog','langsmith','otel']) to include 'langfuse'+'openinference' (sorted: ['datadog','langfuse','langsmith','openinference','otel']) and rename the test title from 'the three connectors' to reflect 5. Until then `node --test tests/connectors.test.js` shows 7/8 with this one expected red.

results.domain: E-ingest: OpenAI Assistants/Responses API coercion in src/audit-ingest.js
results.format_valid: ingestForAudit independently re-verified: sample-audit-report.json (object/findings[]/string) and {} all return well-formed {events,errors,stats} with no throw; {} yields 0 events + 1 error. Tagged Responses -> 2 tool events (send_email function_call + search_web tool_call), model gpt-4.1 preserved, PII-in-args flagged with ssn class, egress set. Untagged Responses recognized via output[] item types -> tool surfaced. Assistants thread.run.step -> 1 tool event lookup_user, assistant_id folded into actor.agent + assistant_id/thread_id on meta, model preserved, args PII flagged. Assistants thread.message (text.value object) -> model event with PII-scanned text. Chat function_call regression still extracted. 11 malformed/truncated inputs never throw. 33/33 independent checks pass. node --check passes.
